source_paper_title: The Complexity of Recognizing Facets for the Knapsack Polytope
context:
Section 5 (Concluding Remarks), page 16 (PDF page 16 / 17)

It is still open whether it can be done in time \(f(K)\cdot \mathrm{poly}(n)\) where \(f\) is some computable function independent of \(n\), i.e., whether the knapsack facet recognition problem is fixed-parameter tractable.
title: Fixed-parameter tractability of knapsack facet recognition parameterized by the number of distinct coefficients
latex:
Given integers \(n\in\mathbb{N}\), \(a\in\mathbb{N}^n\), \(b\in\mathbb{N}\) with \(\max_{i\in[n]} a_i\le b\), define the \((0,1)\) knapsack set
\[
Q(a,b):=\{x\in\{0,1\}^n: a^\top x\le b\}
\]
and its knapsack polytope \(P(a,b):=\mathrm{conv}(Q(a,b))\subseteq\mathbb{R}^n\).
Let \(\alpha\in\mathbb{Z}_+^n\) and \(\beta\in\mathbb{Z}_+\), and define \(K:=\lvert\{\alpha_i: \alpha_i>0,\ i\in[n]\}\rvert\), i.e., the number of distinct positive values among the coefficients of \(\alpha\).
Consider the decision problem:
- Input: \((a,b,\alpha,\beta)\) as above.
- Question: Does the inequality \(\alpha^\top x\le \beta\) define a facet of \(P(a,b)\)?

Problem: Determine whether there exists a computable function \(f:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}\), independent of \(n\), and an algorithm that solves the above decision problem in time \(f(K)\cdot \mathrm{poly}(n,\lVert a,b,\alpha,\beta\rVert)\), where \(\lVert\cdot\rVert\) denotes the input encoding length. Equivalently, is knapsack facet recognition fixed-parameter tractable (FPT) when parameterized by \(K\), the number of distinct positive coefficients in \(\alpha\)?
open_problem_quote: It is still open whether it can be done in time \(f(K)\cdot \mathrm{poly}(n)\) where \(f\) is some computable function independent of \(n\), i.e., whether the knapsack facet recognition problem is fixed-parameter tractable.

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
